examples: With suavity; in a suave manner.
Mozart usually keeps his surfaces smoother than Haydn, achieving a more polished suavity.
The beauty of the best of these wines (like those in the box) is that they carry their weight with a George Saunders-like suavity.
They need to exude suavity, which accounts for the appearance of so many "ultra-thins, " with slim cases that won't withstand the rigors of sport.
